---
title: "Session 7: AI Tools II"
subtitle: "Agentic AI on your desktop (Cowork)"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## This session is under development
The detailed walkthrough will be added before **April 29, 2026**. The outline below mirrors the planned 75-minute structure. The running exercise (scraping the Cornell Dyson PhD placement page with Cowork) picks up from [Session 6](session6.qmd) and continues into [Session 8](session8.qmd).
:::

## Recap and safety brief (~10 min) {#recap-and-safety}

- Mode A vs Mode B, carried over from Session 6.
- What changes with Cowork: it can see your files, run code on your machine, and modify files on disk.
- Git is the safety net. Every exercise starts with a clean commit and ends with another.
- Cowork permissions walk-through: which folder is shared, how to see what the agent has done, how to stop a session.
- Three-minute setup check on the floor.

## Cowork as a category {#cowork-as-a-category}

- What Cowork is: chat plus file access plus code execution.
- Current examples in the category: Cowork, ChatGPT with desktop integration, Gemini in Workspace. The category name ("agentic desktop AI") matters more than the product name.
- The shift from chat: context becomes automatic, action happens in your environment, audit trail has three sources (session log, file changes, git diff).
- Mode B discipline still applies. The artifact is the R script, not the session.
- What Cowork is good at: multi-step tasks with intermediate state, debugging with full context, code that uses your actual data shape.
- What Cowork is bad at: anything that cannot be verified by reading the output, production pipelines, and work where the hidden reasoning ends up in your paper.

## Guided scrape of Cornell Dyson placements (~30 min) {#guided-scrape}

- Target: [Cornell Dyson PhD placements page](https://dyson.cornell.edu/programs/graduate/placements/).
- Goal output: `placements_dyson.csv` with columns `name`, `year`, `placement`, `source_url`.
- A shared prompt handout, including explicit Mode B enforcement.
- Instructor live demo first, then students repeat on their laptops.
- Verification checklist, repeated from Session 6.
- Commit point: everyone commits before moving on.

## Stress test: add a field (~10 min) {#stress-test}

- Ask Cowork to modify the working script to add one of: `is_postdoc`, `country`, or `placement_sector`.
- What to watch for: silent row loss, silent row gain, column reordering that breaks downstream code.
- Two students share what broke. Instructor writes the pattern on the board.

## Debrief and preview of Session 8 {#debrief}

- What worked, what did not. Highlight verification catches.
- Why this was manageable: one page, one department, clean HTML.
- Why Session 8 will be different: four more departments, four more HTML structures, classification of each placement.
- Three-modes table, now with a lived experience for mode 2.
- For Monday: install Claude Code (instructions posted), push today's repo to GitHub, think about which applied econ departments you would scrape if you could.
